hero.question: Will the DHS shutdown end after March 31, 2026?
hero.prob_take: The 39% bid says the crowd thinks a DHS shutdown lasting past March 31 is unlikely, but that's pricing in political dysfunction while ignoring Congress's track record of last-minute continuing resolutions that kick the can further down the road. Watch for mid-March budget rhetoric and whether either party actually walks away from negotiations, because a shutdown that holds this long is a tail risk event requiring both parties to genuinely break.

daily_take.headline: DHS Shutdown Odds Jump 10 Points. Is The Market Pricing In Capitulation?
daily_take.deck: DHS shutdown resolution odds jumped from 29% to 39% in 24 hours, signaling traders smell a deal. But the math doesn't add up. March 31 is 14 months away and political leverage shifts constantly. The move looks like panic buying on noise, not signal. Smart money is selling this bounce.

sidebar.headline: Iran war odds flatlined while DHS chaos soared. Market thinks military escalation is contained. Wrong read on Trump's second-term risk tolerance.
sidebar.label: US Forces Enter Iran: 19.5%

sidebar.headline: Arizona's tournament odds ticked up 2.2 points on exactly nothing. Duke and Kansas own the West. This is sucker money.
sidebar.label: Arizona NCAA Title: 20.5%

sidebar.headline: Anduril IPO collapsed 22 points in a day. Defense tech fundraising just got harder. But the company's valuation and runway argue this odds drop overshot.
sidebar.label: Anduril IPO Before 2027: 29.5%
